question: [File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `nodeCertHash` field returned by the query (using `convert_from(abe.node_cert_hash, 'UTF8')`) to obtain TLS certificate hashes for all nodes, enabling certificate pinning bypass attacks that allow MITM interception of fee-related communications?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `minNodeId` greater than `maxNodeId` to `findNetworkNodes()` to trigger the `abe.node_id >= :minNodeId and abe.node_id <= :maxNodeId` condition to always be false, causing an empty result that makes fee calculation code use stale cached fee data outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `publicKey` field returned by the query (from `abe.public_key`) to obtain the RSA public keys of all nodes, enabling cryptographic analysis to identify weak keys on nodes with high `reward_rate` values and target those nodes for fee manipulation?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `stake` field (from `ns.stake`) returned by the query to identify nodes with consensus weight near the `minStake` threshold, then stake just enough to push a node above or below the threshold and alter its effective `reward_rate` fee contribution?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `associatedRegisteredNodes` field (from `n.associated_registered_nodes`) returned by the query to map the relationship between address book nodes and registered nodes, then use this mapping to identify fee parameter inconsistencies between the two node registries?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send concurrent requests with different `fileId` values to `findNetworkNodes()` to create a race condition in the `latest_address_book` CTE evaluation, causing inconsistent `reward_rate` data to be returned during address book updates and enabling fee parameter confusion?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that `latest_node_stake` uses `max(consensus_timestamp)` globally (not per-node) to identify nodes whose stake data was last updated at a different time than the global maximum, causing those nodes to return NULL `reward_rate` values that downstream fee logic treats as zero fees?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `orderDirection` as an empty string to `findNetworkNodes()` to cause both CASE branches in `case when :orderDirection = 'ASC' then abe.node_id end asc, case when :orderDirection = 'DESC' then abe.node_id end desc` to evaluate to NULL, resulting in undefined ordering that exposes fee data in an unpredictable sequence?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `address_book_service_endpoint` subquery joining on `abse.consensus_timestamp = abe.consensus_timestamp and abse.node_id = abe.node_id` to infer the exact consensus timestamp of the current address book, then use this to correlate fee parameter changes with specific consensus events?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `fileId` as a very large number (e.g., `Long.MAX_VALUE`) to `findNetworkNodes()` to cause the `where file_id = :fileId` clause to return no address book, resulting in an empty `latest_address_book` CTE that causes the main query to return no rows and disrupts fee schedule availability?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `rewardRateStart` field (mapped from `ns.reward_rate`) to observe per-node reward rates and identify nodes where `reward_rate` deviates significantly from the network average, indicating potential fee parameter manipulation that has already occurred outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `left join node_info n on abe.node_id = n.node_id` left join to identify nodes present in `address_book_entry` but absent from the `node` table, indicating nodes with potentially unvalidated fee parameters that bypass the `node` table's fee constraints?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `nodeCertHash` CASE expression `case when abe.node_cert_hash is null or abe.node_cert_hash = ''::bytea then '0x'` to identify nodes with missing or empty certificate hashes, indicating nodes with weakened security that may be more susceptible to fee parameter tampering?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `nodeIds` containing `null` elements to `findNetworkNodes()` to cause unexpected behavior in the `abe.node_id = any(:nodeIds)` PostgreSQL array comparison, potentially causing a query error or returning unintended rows with manipulated fee data?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `stakeNotRewarded` field (from `nullif(ns.stake_not_rewarded, -1)`) to identify nodes where a large proportion of stake is not rewarded, then use this to calculate the effective fee rate reduction and identify nodes operating outside normal fee design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `fileId` parameter defaulting to 102 in the service layer to observe that requests without `fileId` always query address book 102, then pass `fileId=101` to compare fee parameters between address books and identify discrepancies that indicate out-of-design fee modifications?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `grpcProxyEndpoint` JSON field extraction `coalesce(n.grpc_proxy_endpoint->>'domain_name', '')` to enumerate all gRPC proxy domain names, then use DNS manipulation against those domains to redirect fee-related gRPC traffic to attacker-controlled endpoints?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `endConsensusTimestamp` field (from `ab.end_consensus_timestamp`) returned by the query to determine when the current address book expires, then time fee-manipulation transactions to occur just before the address book update when fee parameters are in transition?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `minNodeId=0` and `maxNodeId=Long.MAX_VALUE` with `limit=25` and `orderDirection='DESC'` to always retrieve the 25 highest-ID nodes, potentially targeting recently added nodes that may have non-standard `reward_rate` fee parameters not yet validated against design constraints?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `publicKey` CASE expression `case when abe.public_key is null or abe.public_key = '' then '0x'` to identify nodes with missing public keys, indicating nodes that may have bypassed key validation and could have unauthorized fee parameter modifications?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `memo` field (from `abe.memo`) returned by the query to observe node account IDs embedded in memos, then cross-reference these with on-chain transactions to identify fee parameter update transactions submitted by node operators?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `description` field (from `abe.description`) returned by the query to extract metadata about nodes that may reveal fee configuration details or version information indicating nodes running non-standard fee parameter configurations?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `startConsensusTimestamp` field (from `ab.start_consensus_timestamp`) returned by the query to determine the exact timestamp when the current fee parameters took effect, enabling precise timing of transactions to exploit the window between old and new fee schedules?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user send a high-frequency polling attack against `findNetworkNodes()` with varying `fileId` values to detect the exact moment when a new address book with updated fee parameters is published, enabling front-running of fee parameter changes?

[File: 'File Name: rest-java/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/restjava/repository/NetworkNodeRepository.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: findNetworkNodes()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `nodeAccountId` field (from `coalesce(n.account_id, abe.node_account_id)`) to identify the account IDs of all network nodes, then monitor those accounts for balance changes that indicate fee collection activity outside normal design parameters?
